summary.intent: Review Greptile and other PR comments on moltis PR #440 (fix-heartbeat-cron-job-creation), fix the outstanding issues, and resolve the conversations.
summary.outcome: Fixed two code issues (job lookup by id instead of name, disabled-job creation guard), added a new test for the enabled=false+prompt case, all 4 tests pass, and changes were committed.

repo: Heartbeat cron job logic lives in crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs around lines 1660-1730
Integration tests for heartbeat cron are in crates/gateway/tests/heartbeat_cron.rs
The project requires a Tailwind CSS build step (npm deps + tailwind build) before Rust tests can compile successfully
The mock CronJob store in tests must explicitly apply patch fields (schedule, payload, enabled, delete_after_run, sandbox, wake_mode) to stored state for state-based assertions to work

code.finding: Job lookup was incorrectly using j.name instead of j.id, causing inconsistent matching
Condition was `else if has_prompt` which would create a cron job even when enabled=false; corrected to `else if effective_enabled` so disabled jobs are never pre-staged
futures::{SinkExt, StreamExt} import was previously placed mid-function; now correctly at top-level use block
Mock update handler applies all patch fields to stored CronJob before returning success, enabling correct state-based assertions

workflow: Greptile review comments can be fetched via GitHub API and cross-referenced against current code to determine resolution status before making changes
When a CSS asset build is missing, Rust integration tests fail at compile time with a missing file error rather than a linker or test error
Running tests in the specific crate (cargo test -p gateway) is faster and more targeted than running workspace-wide tests

friction: Rust test build failed mid-run due to a missing Tailwind CSS file, requiring an npm install + CSS build step before tests could proceed — this dependency is non-obvious
Initial test run output was truncated, requiring a second run to surface the actual error
open_items: PR conversations were to be resolved via GitHub API after the commit — transcript ends before confirming that step completed

The comment explaining 'create disabled when prompt exists' behavior was added but the final condition change removes that case entirely; the comment may now be stale or misleading and should be reviewed
